channel.title: HistoryPulse — The Uncut Story
channel.description: Deep-dive uncut narrations from real historical sources.
channel.itunes:author: HistoryPulse

item.title: Blood on the Dance Floor: Assassination, Invasion, and a King Undone by Love — Vijayanagara Empire Chapter 3
item.description: In this chapter, we witness the brutal succession struggles of the Bahmani Sultanate — where boy-kings are blinded within weeks of taking the throne — and the dramatic assassination of Bukka II's son by a Bahmani spy named Cauzi Serauje, who infiltrated the Hindu camp disguised as a musician during a night entertainment, charmed the prince with his playing, then plunged daggers into his breast during a knife dance. The ensuing chaos allowed Sultan Firuz Shah to cross the flooded Krishna River and rout the Vijayanagar army, leading to a humiliating peace treaty. We then follow the reckless King Deva Raya I, whose obsessive desire for a farmer's beautiful daughter named Pertal — a girl who courageously refused his proposal — drove him to launch a military raid into Bahmani territory, sparking a catastrophic war that ended with the unprecedented disgrace of giving a Hindu princess in marriage to a Muslim sultan. Based on A Forgotten Empire (Vijayanagara) by Robert Sewell (1900).

item.title: Blood on the Tungabhadra: When Total War Came to Vijayanagara
item.description: Sultan Muhammad Shah Bahmani launches total war against Vijayanagara in 1366, inflicting catastrophic defeats on Bukka Raya — including a massacre at the Tungabhadra battlefield where even pregnant women and infants were slaughtered, and a devastating night attack that caught the king drinking with dancing-women — ultimately claiming 500,000 Hindu lives before peace is bought. Years later, the young Sultan Mujahid penetrates to the very heart of the capital and destroys a sacred jeweled temple, only to be assassinated by his own uncle Daud Khan, whose treachery gives Bukka the opening to reclaim the disputed Doab territories. Based on "A Forgotten Empire (Vijayanagara)" by Robert Sewell (1900).

item.title: The City of Victory: How Two Captured Brothers Built an Empire from the Ashes — Vijayanagara, Chapter 1
item.description: In this opening chapter, we trace the cataclysmic events that led to the founding of Vijayanagara around 1336 CE — from the relentless southward advance of the Delhi Sultanate under rulers like Ala-ud-din Khilji and the terrifying Muhammad Taghlaq, through the fall of the Hindu fortress of Kampli-Anegundi, to the rise of two brothers, Harihara and Bukka, who — guided by the sage Vidyaranya — established a new city on the Tungabhadra River that would become the last great Hindu empire of southern India. Based on A Forgotten Empire (Vijayanagara) by Robert Sewell (1900).
